rulings.Case Title: MARIYA YUKHIMOVA VS. GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Oleg Scamin, Individually And Dba Scol, Co'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 6, 2013, Line 7. Defendant OLEG SCAMIN, individually and dba SCOL, CO.'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED- For good cause shown in the moving papers of OLEG SCAMIN, individually and dba SCOL CO and the Declaration of Peter C. Cassini, M.D., Plaintiff Mariya Yukhimova shall appear for a neurologic examination at the offices of Peter C. Cassini, M.D. located at 326 Bryant Street, Palo Alto, CA 94301. The examination shall take place on June 10, 2013 at 10:00 am. The entire examination shall not last longer than 2 hours and will be conducted by Peter C. Cassini, M.D., a licensed Neurologist. The examination will involve taking a medically relevant history, a general physical neurologic exam looking at everything from cranial nerves to motor function, dexterity and sensory function. These tests are to be done in a painless, passive manner. A more focused, detailed examination may be conducted following the general examination to better identify the source of any specific problems. The examination is not to involve any painful or protracted testing. No X-Rays or any sort of electronic scanning will be performed during the examination. The Plaintiff is ordered to cooperate during the examination so that it may be completed within the 2 hour limitation. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Juventino Estrada'S Motion To Compel Further Written Responses, And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 6, 2013, Line 4 (part 2 of 2). Plaintiff JUVENTINO ESTRADA'S Motion to Compel Further Written Responses, and Production of Documents (Part 2 of 2) The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff JUVENTINO ESTRADA'S Motion to Compel Further Written Responses and Production of Documents, Set Three to Defendant NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURGH, PA ("NATIONAL UNION") is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff seeks supplemental, verified, written responses, and production of all documents responsive to Request 34, 35 and 36 in Plaintiff's Request for Production, Set Three. As to Request 34, Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED subject to certain limitations imposed by the Court. (Greyhound Corp. v. Superior Court (1961) 56 C2d 355, 383-385). NATIONAL UNION is ordered to produce all written guidelines or policies that NATIONAL UNION has provided to Northwest Direct to guide them in receiving telephone calls and speaking with NATIONAL UNION insureds. As to Request 35, Plaintiff's Motion is DENIED. As to Request 36, Plaintiff's Motion is GRANTED, in part. NATIONAL UNION is to provide a complete, verified written response to the Court's modified Request 34 and contemporaneous production of responsive documents within 20 days of Notice of Entry of Order. This Order is made without prejudice to NATIONAL UNION seeking a protective order if there is a valid basis for protection of the documents as trade secret or proprietary. NATIONAL UNION is to provide a complete, verified written response to Request 36 and must produce a complete copy of the Agreement between NATIONAL UNION and Northwest Direct referenced in the deposition of NATIONAL UNION's PMK, Hart, redacted of anything other than written guidelines or policies that NATIONAL UNION has provided to Northwest Direct to guide them in receiving telephone calls and speaking with NATIONAL UNION insureds within 20 days of Notice of Entry of Order. This Order is made without prejudice to NATIONAL UNION seeking a protective order if there is a valid basis for protection of the documents as trade secret or proprietary. No sanctions will be awarded to either party. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST VS. TON KIANG RESTAURANT, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 6, 2013, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT OLIVER J. MAR, TRUSTEE OF THE MAR LIVING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The Court grants Plaintiff's request for judicial notice. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the breach of contract cause of action. Defendant does not allege the contract according to its legal effect. Defendant's breach of contract cause of action is conclusory and does not sufficiently allege how plaintiff is obligated to cure the March 29, 2005 notice of violation. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. The declaratory relief cause of action fails to state a claim in that it incorporates by reference paragraphs 1 through 7, and doesn't sufficiently describe the duties with respect to the easement. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TOM VS. AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 6, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT AAA NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, NEVADA & UTAH INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. In the context of a claim against an insurer for its handling of insured's policy, only bad faith should be the basis of the insured's cause of action. (See Brown v. Guarantee Insurance Co., (1957) 155 Cal.App.2d 679, 689). However, Defendants' demurrer to the "vicarious liability for negligence allegations" is overruled as an improper basis for demurrer. Defendants should have filed a motion to strike. No opposition was filed. rulings.Case Title: KRISTINE PETTERSON VS. UNITEDHEALTHCARE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 6, 2013, Line 23, Defendant's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Granted. Defendants have shown that Plaintiff and Defendants entered into an arbitration agreement. The arbitration agreement covers the disputes at issue in the lawsuit. Defendants demanded arbitration and Plaintiff refused to arbitrate. The agreement is valid, enforceable and not unconscionable. See Dotson v. Amgen, Inc. (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 975; Serpa v. California Surety Investigations, Inc. (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 695; Wilson v. United Health Group, Inc. 2012 WL 6088318 (E.D. Cal. 2012). Plaintiff is ordered to submit her claims to arbitration. The proceedings in this Court are stayed pending completion of arbitration or further order of the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INC., VS. KC & FAMILY LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 6, 2013, Line 19, PLAINTIFF SYSCO SAN FRANCISCO INC.'s Application For Right To Attach Order and Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment. Denied. Plaintiff relies on the verified complaint and Ableman Declaration in support of this application. (Application, Item 7(a), 7(b).) First, the Complaint is not verified. Assuming in arguendo that Plaintiff filed a verified complaint, a verified complaint cannot support an application for right to attach order where the plaintiff is a corporation. (CCP § 446; see Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial, 9:912.) Second, the Ableman Declaration is a boilerplate declaration that does not aver facts with sufficient particularity to support this application. (CCP § 482.040.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the claims against Ms. Castillo arises out of her trade, business or profession. (CCP § 483.010(c).) Third, the Credit Application is not attached to the Exhibit C to the Ableman Declaration. (Ableman Decl., 9.) To the extent that Plaintiff filed a single application to attach property against a limited liability company and a natural person, Plaintiff is instructed to file separate applications. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA PACE VS. TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Toc Ompel Claimant'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Document Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, June 6, 2013, Line 5. Defendant TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE (erroneously sued as FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE)'s Motion to Compel Claimant's Further Responses to Form Interrogatories and Document Production Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Respondent, TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion to Compel Claimant's Further Responses to Form Interrogatories and Document Production is GRANTED. As a threshold issue, the motion is timely and venue is correct. Claimant, JOHANNA PACE must provide verified, complete and straightforward supplemental responses to form interrogatory 2.13, 6.7, 10.1, 12.4. The complete, straightforward, supplemental verified responses, are due within 20 calendar days of the date of service of the Notice of Entry of Order on this motion. Claimant is to provide a complete, verified written response to Respondents Request for Production of Documents, Set One, No. 1, 2 and 3 and contemporaneous production of responsive documents, at no cost to Respondent, bates stamped, ordered and labeled to correspond with the categories in the demand as set forth in Code of Civil Procedure §2031.280(a) within 20 days of Notice of Entry of Order. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com or a fax to 415.391.8766 with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
